title: Research on Expressive Writing in Psychology: A Forty-year Bibliometric Analysis and Visualization of Current Status and Research Trends
population: 1,429 expressive writing publications from the Web of Science Core Collection
main_findings: The paper maps the expressive writing field over four decades rather than testing an intervention effect directly.
It shows that expressive writing research has become broad, multidisciplinary, and methodologically diverse.
It identifies future directions such as positive psychology contexts and broader population coverage.
allowed_inferences: Use this source to prioritize further reading and identify clusters of expressive writing research.
Use it to justify building a curated evidence base instead of relying on isolated studies.
forbidden_inferences: Do not use this paper as evidence that Aurum improves user outcomes.
Do not convert bibliometric trends into user-facing efficacy claims.
do_not_use_for: prompt grounding for specific emotional analyses
prompt_guidance: None. This card is for research planning, not runtime prompting.
notes: Keep this as context only. It is useful for deciding what evidence to read next, not for grounding individual Aurum reflections.
